All right, so welcome back. In this lesson, I'm going to walk you through what we are doing for the pre-call onboarding. So this comes after they've already filled out the questionnaire, booked their time, and now we're in that in-between between call booking and your call beginning. So the first core asset, the main one you're going to be sending out is quite simply your confirmation email, which is going to reinforce a lot of the elements that were already on the thank you confirmation page. So very simple subject line, very important details about your AI exploration call, plus program access. Remember that part of our confirmation page was giving them immediate access to the first few videos of the main program to coach that sense of momentum. So very simple body, so grateful of you taking the time to answer all those questions. We go pretty deep on those, but it's only because we care. So we're acknowledging the fact that they really did make an investment of time, energy, and attention in filling those out, and we don't take that for granted. The good news is you invested this time and energy to apply for your AI exploration call, and now we're here. Time for the awesomeness to officially begin, so a sense of feeling rewarded for that time and the effort that they've taken. And then once again, we assume that leadership stands of directing what happens next. So here are the very important next steps I'd love for you to consider in advance of our call. So the first one is going to be protecting against no shows, so coaching the show up, right? And the best way to coach the show up is really to develop that magnetism, right? Of being someone that one would not want to go, being one that someone would feel safe showing up in the room with. So defend our time slot like a mom and bear would defend her cubs, or just having fun with that. A good bet would be putting it on your calendar and we have a link in case they haven't already put it on their calendar. And then we just go a little bit deeper on what these calls mean for us. So we go all in on these calls. It's not some low ranked hired gun salesperson taking them. It's us as in filling myself. Next one you can consider doing this could be automated or this could be something that you essentially do if you have low call volume on the fly. But we call this the enchanting first encounter. It's something we go into deeper detail on the automation side inside of automated intimacy, but it's essentially a personalized 45 to 60 second video message that you would send them within eight hours of the application. Right. And we're doing this for a few reasons. We're doing to build connection and magnetism before the call and stating why you're genuinely excited to talk to them. You want to defend once against defend against the no shows by coaching a greater sense of accountability that they're not just anonymously on your calendar, but you see them, you acknowledge them and you're expecting to talk to them and you're reinforcing those next steps and the momentum. So basic format, you can congratulate congratulate them on booking the call. So greet them with the same level of enthusiasm you would a new client or customer. Right. That's a very great frame to approach these videos with repeat the exact time and date of the call. So this becomes very apparent and very real that this isn't just an automated one to many video. You sent everyone who books, but very specific to them. Remind them of any pre homework or elements that you would like them to complete before the call, such as the first few modules of your program. Why are personally excited? So this is an incredible opportunity to mirror back what stood out about their application. What are you excited and honored to be able to serve? Right. The more you can mirror back what you appreciate in them in a genuine, sincere way, you're going to be coaching that relationship and the magnetism within it. And then of course, accountability, right? If they can't make it and making sure they give you that level of advanced notice. So that is how it would play out in what we call an enchanting first encounter.
